_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use reading strategies — like visualising, predicting, connecting, summarising and questioning — to understand texts better.

_source.w: We are learning to use reading strategies — like visualising, predicting, connecting, summarising and questioning — to understand texts better.
y: Active readers do not just read words — they think while they read.
These strategies help us understand and remember more of what we read.
The more strategies we use, the deeper our understanding becomes.
eg: We close our eyes and visualise a scene from a story while someone reads it aloud.
We predict what will happen next based on clues in the text.
We connect something in the text to our own experience or another book.
We summarise a chapter in one or two sentences.
We ask questions about the text — 'Why did the character do that?'
We use our learnt vocabulary to help us understand difficult parts of a text.
